title: A joint resolution providing for congressional disapproval of the proposed foreign military sale to Israel of certain defense articles and services.
plain_summary: This bill would stop the United States from selling weapons to Israel. The weapons include ten thousand pieces of military equipment like explosive parts and artillery supplies. Senator Sanders introduced this bill because he wants Congress to block this weapons sale from happening. If the bill passes, the US government would not be allowed to sell these defense items to Israel.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: This joint resolution was introduced by Senator Sanders on February twentieth, twenty twenty-five. It was read twice and sent to the Committee on Foreign Relations. The joint resolution stops Congress from approving a planned military sale to Israel. Specifically, it blocks the sale of ten thousand pieces of artillery equipment called one hundred fifty-five millimeter ancillaries. These ancillaries include fuzes (devices that trigger explosions), primers (devices that start explosions), and charges (explosive materials). These items would be added to weapons cases that were already approved before. The sale was described in a document called Transmittal Number twenty-four dash sixteen. This document was sent to Congress following the Arms Export Control Act, which is a law that requires the government to tell Congress about planned weapons sales to other countries. The details of this sale were published in the Congressional Record on February tenth, twenty twenty-five.
